#  **System Model H1** Accelerating your path to fully fault-tolerant quantum computing
Get Started
[Get Started](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/system-model-h1-series#access)
## Meet our first system 
## propelling quantum computing **forward**
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/669960f53cd73aedb80c8eea/66c20cb965fa641f874dd8d3_model%20h1.svg)
**System Model H1** is our first-generation of quantum computers with a single linear architecture and numerous hallmark features that set it apart from other types of quantum computers, including:
  * Highest commercially available two-qubit gate fidelity
  * All-to-all-connectivity
  * Mid-circuit measurement
  * Conditional logic
  * Qubit reuse


[explore technical resources](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1#h1-resources)
### 20
fully-connected qubits
### 1,048,576 (220)
quantum volume
### 99.998%
single-qubit gate fidelity
### >99.9%
two-qubit gate fidelity
## Projects run on **System Model H1**
Genons, Double Covers and Fault-tolerant Clifford Gates
Our team unlocked a powerful approach to fault tolerance - finding a new way to execute fault tolerant gates when using exotic error correcting codes that offer more logical qubits per physical qubit.
[Read the scientific paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.09951)[Read our blogpost](https://www.quantinuum.com/blog/quantinuum-researchers-are-unlocking-a-more-efficient-and-powerful-path-towards-fault-tolerance)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)
Nonlinear dynamics as a ground-state solution on quantum computers
A joint team of scientists at Quantinuum, the German Aerospace Center, the Helmhotlz Institute and Ulm University developed a new approach to solving Partial Differential Equations (PDEs), which has impacts across every industry imaginable.
[Read the scientific paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.16791)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)
Playing nonlocal games across a topological phase transition on a quantum computer
Quantinuum researchers teamed up with CUBoulder, CalTech and University of Sydney, New South Wales to play “non-local quantum games” on our H-1 quantum computer, which may shed light on materials physics.
[Read the scientific paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.04829)[Read our blogpost](https://www.quantinuum.com/publications/playing-nonlocal-games-across-a-topological-phase-transition-on-a-quantum-computer)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)
Comparison of current quantum devices for quantum computing of Heisenberg spin chain dynamics
Researchers simulated a “Heisenberg spin chain”, which is a model that can help us understand magnetism, on both our H1-1 processor and on an IBM processor.
[Read the scientific paper](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0009261423006802)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)
Protecting expressive circuits with a quantum error detection code
Our scientists developed an ultra-efficient error detecting code, opening the door to early fault-tolerant quantum computing.
[Read the scientific paper](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41567-023-02282-2)[read our blogpost](https://www.quantinuum.com/blog/protecting-expressive-circuits-with-a-quantum-error-detection-code)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)
Peptide Binding Classification on Quantum Computers
Our researchers used the H1-1 processor to perform a task in computational biology, finding competitive performance compared to classical baselines of similar scale.
[Read the scientific paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.15696)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)[](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/quantinuum-systems/system-model-h1)
##  **Start your journey** with the world’s highest-performing quantum computers
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/669960f53cd73aedb80c8eea/66fd5e43608b868463139448_quantinuumsystem_updatedsize.svg)
Purchase a subscription directly with Quantinuum to access our trapped-ion quantum computers, the world’s leading quantum hardware
[Start with direct access](https://www.quantinuum.com/contact/quantinuum-systems)
Perform quantum chemistry research with InQuanto, state of the art platform utilizing Quantinuum systems for complex molecular and materials simulations
[start with an inquanto license](https://www.quantinuum.com/products-solutions/inquanto-partner-residency)
Purchase a subscription on Microsoft Azure with access to Quantinuum Systems
[start with Microsoft Azure](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/quantum/provider-quantinuum)
Researchers in the United States may apply for a quantum credits grant on Quantinuum systems through their Quantum Computing User Program (QCUP)
[start with ORNL](https://www.olcf.ornl.gov/olcf-resources/compute-systems/quantum-computing-user-program/quantum-computing-user-support-documentation/)
System Model H1 **Resources**
[Performance Validation](https://docs.quantinuum.com/systems/user_guide/hardware_user_guide/performance_validation.html)[Quantum Volume Data](https://docs.quantinuum.com/systems/user_guide/hardware_user_guide/benchmarks/system_benchmarks.html)[Citing Quantinuum Systems](https://docs.quantinuum.com/systems/support/quantinuum_systems_citations.html)
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/669960f53cd73aedb80c8eea/66c28e55227c13f211ad34e0_tc_resources.png)
